competitor.name: AutoPilot Bistro
competitor.tagline: Your menu. Your neighborhood. Zero downtime.

threat_analysis: An AI-native clone of your business opens across the street. Within 48 hours it has replied to every online review, launched a 3-post-per-week Instagram strategy, set up automated invoice reminders, and built a simple online ordering page. It never forgets a follow-up, never misses a supplier deadline, and never lets a negative review sit unanswered. It runs the same business you do — just without the blind spots.

week_one_actions.action: Responded to all 8 unreplied reviews within 10 minutes of posting — warm, personal, owner-signed.
week_one_actions.impact: Customers who get a reply are 68% more likely to return and update their rating.

week_one_actions.action: Posted 3 times on Instagram with behind-the-scenes prep shots, customer shoutouts, and a weekend special teaser.
week_one_actions.impact: Consistent posting builds a 2–3x follower growth rate over businesses that post sporadically.

week_one_actions.action: Sent friendly payment reminders on day 1, day 7, and day 14 for all outstanding invoices — personalized per client.
week_one_actions.impact: Automated follow-ups recover 40% of overdue payments within the first week.

week_one_actions.action: Generated a Monday morning brief with the week's priorities, deadlines, and unresolved items — delivered before 7am.
week_one_actions.impact: Owner starts every day knowing exactly what to do first, instead of reacting to whatever's loudest.

week_one_actions.action: Flagged 3 at-risk customers based on negative reviews and sent personal follow-up offers.
week_one_actions.impact: Recovering just 2 churned customers per month adds $200–400 in recurring revenue.

pop_recommendation: You don't need to become TechBro Tacos. You need a Chief of Staff that handles the stuff you keep putting off — the reviews, the invoices, the posts, the Monday morning plan. Pop does that. Same warmth, same voice, same you — just with nothing falling through the cracks.
